biographical_info.caption: Dynasty Warriors: Origins render
biographical_info.force: Guardians of Peace, Wei, Wu, Shu

sections.heading: Lead
sections.content: Wanderer (onyomi: Mumei) is the protagonist of Dynasty Warriors: Origins. As the story progresses, however, characters compare his skill and importance to his allies, he is nicknamed Ziluan as an ode to the legendary bird by his close friends.

sections.heading: History
sections.content:
Ziluan was one of the best warriors born into the Guardians of Peace, a small village committed to maintaining the peace of the land. This eventually results in conflict with the Han government when Ziluan, Zhuhe, and Bailuan refuse to execute an innocent baby. This leads to the imperial forces under Wang Yun attacking the village, killing Zhuhe as Ziluan and Bailuan escape with the others. The trauma from the attack results in the Ziluan losing his memories, becoming a lone vigilante as the Han government grew increasingly corrupt. While traversing north, the Wanderer steps in when poor peasants are oppressed by the local official. This results in the official calling his guards against the player, but he is soon assisted by a red-faced warrior and a bearded man. While the Wanderer and the warrior deal with the initial guards, the bearded man calls his followers, the Yellow Turbans, and helps the player demolish the official's garrison before the three part ways.

The warrior soon introduces himself to the Wanderer as Guan Yu, a man looking to restore peace to the land. The two split up, but as the Yellow Turbans rise to power, the uncontrolled greed of its members makes them little better than ruffians and bandits. Seeing this, the Wanderer reunites with Guan Yu, having now sworn an oath of brotherhood with Liu Bei and Zhang Fei. The three brothers recruit the Wanderer into their volunteer army in the hopes of quelling the Yellow Turban Rebellion.

As the volunteer army makes a name for themselves, aiding the likes of Cao Cao, Sun Jian, and Dong Zhuo, the player and Guan Yu reunite with the bearded man, who is none other than Zhang Jiao, the leader of the Yellow Turbans. Realizing the Wanderer was a Guardian of Peace, Zhang Jiao challenges the Wanderer to a final duel to determine the righteousness of his cause. After slaying Zhang Bao and Zhang Liang, the Wanderer finally defeats Zhang Jiao, who escapes Dong Zhuo and Huangfu Song's pursuing forces. The two escape to the ruins of the Guardians of Peace's village and Zhang Jiao asks the Wanderer why he was unable to control his followers and where he had erred. When the Wanderer is unable to answer, Zhang Jiao passes away, content that his actions had at least served to jump start an age of peace. With the land still in chaos, the Wanderer returns to the village and meets Yuanhua, a young medical student and one of Zhang Jiao's acquaintances. Upon realizing the Wanderer was suffering amnesia, Yuanhua decides to become his companion until a cure can be found.

Though the Yellow Turbans were scattered, corrupt officials in Luoyang remained, and the Wanderer intervenes when he sees assassins going after a woman and her father. After being rescued, the pair introduce themselves as Diaochan and Wang Yun. Wang Yun explains to the Wanderer that he hoped to gather allied forces against the Ten Eunuchs as their corruption and influence over the Emperor resulted in the deteriorating administration of the government. The Wanderer accompanies Wang Yun as they are attacked by assassins on the trip to Luoyang. Fortunately, forces from Cao Cao and Dong Zhuo, having been called by Wang Yun, arrive to guarantee their safe passage to the capital, though Dong Zhuo keeps his frontier forces outside the city.

Having worked the Wanderer to be a Guardian of Peace, Wang Yun sends the player with Diaochan, Cao Cao, Yuan Shao, and Yuan Shu to the capital to assist He Jin in arresting the Ten Eunuchs. To their surprise, He Jin was ambushed and killed while the eunuchs were now attempting to take the Emperor away. Though the Wanderer and his allies slay the eunuchs, they are unable to find the Emperor anywhere in the city. To their horror, the Emperor returns in the custody of Dong Zhuo, who abuses his position as the eunuchs previously had. At Xun You's urging, Wang Yun finally acts and sends the Wanderer with Cao Cao and Diaochan to try and assassinate Dong Zhuo. This fails when his adoptive son Lu Bu arrives and surrounds the trio. While the Wanderer and Cao Cao escape, both Diaochan and Wang Yun remain in the capital and are forced to swear fealty to Dong Zhuo.

Requiring allies to take this new threat, Cao Cao brings over Yuan Shao and issues a missive calling on the other governors to stand against Dong Zhuo. The Wanderer reunites with Liu Bei and Sun Jian's forces as they engage Dong Zhuo's forces at both Sishui Gate and Hulao Gate. Despite their best efforts, Dong Zhuo burns Luoyang and escapes to Chang'an with the Emperor in tow. Soon after, he is assassinated by Lu Bu and Diaochan, resulting in the coalition of warlords immediately fragmenting and bickering among one another.

If the Wanderer chooses to follow Liu Bei, he will join Liu Bei in defending Xu Province after Cao Cao launches a punitive attack to avenge his father. This attack is repelled when Lu Bu attacks Cao Cao's territory in the rear. Indebted to Lu Bu, the player and Liu Bei rush to save the general when he is cornered by Yuan Shao and Cao Cao's forces, offering Lu Bu sanctuary at Xu Province. In spite of their kindness, Lu Bu betrays them and takes the province for himself, forcing Liu Bei to be his subordinate instead.

At this time, Cao Cao sends out a request for aid against Yuan Shu, who has falsely declared himself emperor. The player and Liu Bei are compelled to join Lu Bu in accompanying Cao Cao and Sun Ce in besieging Yuan Shu at Shouchun. With Yuan Shu defeated, the group returns back to Xiaopei. Before long, Lu Bu attacks Liu Bei's forces again, who are forced to request aid from Cao Cao to escape safely. As the Wanderer and Liu Bei reunite with Cao Cao once more, Liu Bei asks why Cao Cao had intentionally tricked Lu Bu into believing they had betrayed him, only to be brushed off as a means to help them be rid of Lu Bu's barbarism.

Although unhappy at having been unwillingly dragged into conflict in this manner, Liu Bei and the Wanderer nonetheless join Cao Cao's campaign against Lu Bu, successfully slaying him at Xia Pi. Unable to deal with Cao Cao's influence, the emperor sends a private message to Liu Bei asking him to help eliminate Cao Cao. Though Liu Bei doesn't act on this, the letter is discovered and he and Zhang Fei are forced to flee to Yuan Shao while leaving the Wanderer and Guan Yu to defend Xiapi and cover their escape. Surrounded at all sides, Cao Cao convinces the pair to surrender and stay alive in exchange for the opportunity to return to Liu Bei in the future.

Though Cao Cao is happy to have both men in his service, the Wanderer and Guan Yu make their service short, only aiding him in the opening skirmish against Yuan Shao's forces at Baima and Yanjin. In this skirmish, the Wanderer comes face-to-face with Bailuan, who immediately recognizes the Wanderer as Ziluan, and attacks him for aiding Cao Cao and the Han emperor. After defeating Bailuan, Ziluan and Guan Yu slay Yuan Shao's top generals, Yan Liang and Wen Chou. As they rendered Cao Cao a great deed and Liu Bei's location was reported, Xun Yu allows the two to depart. As soon as they join Liu Bei, however, the group find themselves surrounded and attacked by the strategist as they flee south to Liu Biao's territory.

After evading Cao Cao, the group recruit Xu Shu as a strategist when Xiahou Yuan stations his troops against them at Xinye. The group are able to claim victory, but Xu Shu feels that his merits were inadequate and leaves Liu Bei, directing him to the more talented Zhuge Liang. Liu Bei's talk with the hermit leads to him lending his intellect to their cause with Cao Cao launching an attack on them at Bowanpo. Thanks to Zhuge Liang's plans, the group defeat Xiahou Dun. This defeat only results in Cao Cao personally leading the attack, forcing the group to flee Changban heading further south.

Confused to see Ziluan fighting against Cao Cao, Bailuan chooses to assist the Ziluan by conjuring a fog to distract Cao Cao's forces in the escape attempt. Knowing that Ziluan's choice of Liu Bei to be more than just blind faith, Bailuan turns against Ziluan and the pair duel, with Ziluan emerging the victor. Convinced, Bailuan parts ways with Ziluan, choosing to allow Ziluan to do as he saw fit to bring peace. With Liu Biao dead and Liu Cong inching to surrender to Cao Cao, Liu Bei joins Sun Quan and engages Cao Cao's forces in a grand battle at Chibi, devastating his fleet in a large fire attack. With Cao Cao on the run, Zhuge Liang and Ziluan lead the chase with multiple ambushes stalling Cao Cao's escape. Despite the warlord being blocked from his exit by both Ziluan and Guan Yu, the latter has Ziluan step aside. This serves as both an act of mercy, and a counterbalance to Sun Quan's inevitable rise to power as they would have their own ambitions afterwards.

In Cao Cao's story, the player enlists in the warlord's army in time to answer Bao Xin's call for aid against Yellow Turbans at Yan Province. As Bao Xin was killed in action, the province gives itself to Cao Cao as hordes of Yellow Turbans, disheartened by the Wanderer fighting against them, surrender en masse. After gaining Yan Province, Cao Cao requests his father to move to his new base, but his escorts, men from Tao Qian, murder Cao Cao's father and flee back to Xu Province. Willing to use his father's death to further his position, Cao Cao and the Wanderer attack Xu Province, destroying much of its forces even with Liu Bei's reinforcements before an attack by Lu Bu at Yan forces them to pull back. Cao Cao regroups with Xun Yu at Puyang to learn that Chen Gong had betrayed them and tasks the Wanderer with engaging Lu Bu's forces and taking back the surrounding bases. In spite of their hard fight, Chen Gong severs their supply line, forcing them to starve or withdraw.

With their granary compromised, Cao Cao's forces head west to the neighboring Yu Province, which also requested for aid against Yellow Turban remnants. The intervention of Cao Cao's forces not only nets them the grateful province and its storehouses, but also Xu Zhu. With his supplies now rejuvenated, the Wanderer joins the second attack on Yan Province, finally expelling Lu Bu and forcing him east to Xu Province. With Lu Bu dealt with, Cao Cao receives a message from the west with the Emperor fleeing Li Jue and Guo Si's control. Unable to miss this golden opportunity, the Wanderer is returned to Hulao Gate to rescue the Emperor and deal with both remnants of Dong Zhuo's forces and other would-be caretakers. Now having Emperor Xian to legitimize his acts, Cao Cao receives the surrender of Zhang Xiu near Luoyang, but this turns out to be a ruse as the warlord finds himself trapped inside Wan Castle. Acting quickly, Guo Jia, Xiahou Dun and the Wanderer rush to eliminate exterior support for the assassins before saving Cao Cao from death.

Following this, Cao Cao defends the emperor's prestige by issuing a missive to the nearby warlords to eliminate Yuan Shu, who now declared himself emperor. This leads to the Wanderer reuniting with Liu Bei in better terms though he had been betrayed after sheltering Lu Bu and was now a subordinate to the general. The allied forces crush Yuan Shu at Shouchun, and Cao Cao triumphantly returned home. Hoping to deal with Lu Bu next, Cao Cao sends a fake letter to Liu Bei, knowing that Chen Gong's ambition would cause him to attack Liu Bei at Xiaopei rather than confirm the letter's contents. As planned, the Wanderer is then sent to Xiaopei to evacuate Liu Bei's forces as they soon combined their men and marched against Lu Bu at Xiapi.

Having destroyed Lu Bu, the Wanderer soon learns of a conspiracy against Cao Cao by the Emperor's officials. Learning that Liu Bei was involved, the Wanderer is sent to Xu Province to defeat Guan Yu and retake the province. Although Guan Yu wishes to fight to the death, the Wanderer and Zhang Liao convince him to stand down and join them until he learns of his brother's whereabouts. During this time, Cao Cao asks the Wanderer to try and convince Guan Yu to join them permanently, but both the Wanderer and Guan Yu refuse to do so on account of the latter's honor. Nonetheless, the forces engage Yuan Shao at Baima and Yanjin, with the Wanderer being called out by Bailuan as Ziluan for aiding the bloodline that exterminated their village.

Though losing his top two generals, Yuan Shao still brought his massive force against Cao Cao at Guandu, but Ziluan helps destroy the warlord's supply compound at Wuchao, negating this advantage and defeating Yuan Shao, who would die in disgrace shortly after. With Yuan Shao's sons bickering among one another, Cao Cao launches a campaign north working with Yuan Tan against Yuan Shang. In an attempt to disrupt the allied forces, Bailuan releases a heavy fog and tries to assassinate Cao Cao but is thwarted by Ziluan. Though most cities at Ye were now under them, Guo Jia pushes the forces further north to destroy Yuan Shao's bloodline and end any possibility of rebellion. Ziluan joins Cao Cao's forces in this decisive campaign as Bailuan stalls their sneak attack on Tadun's forces. When this fails to prevent Yuan Shang's defeat, Bailuan finally challenges Ziluan to prove his convictions for Cao Cao. Ziluan eventually defeats Bailuan again, and the latter opts to leave the fate of the land to the one whom Ziluan placed his beliefs on.

With all northern rivals gone, Cao Cao's forces turn south and attack Liu Bei, failing to kill him at Bowanpo and Changban thanks to the tactics of Zhuge Liang. With Liu Bei joining Sun Quan against them, Ziluan joins the final battle against the allied forces at Chibi. Unfortunately, Huang Gai and Pang Tong's betrayal cripples the fleet as the fire attack destroys Cao Cao's navy and forces them to flee in a scattered mess. Regrouping with Cao Cao, the pair defeat Guan Yu in a final duel at Huarong Trail, and he admits that while he held back as a show of mercy and honor, he still had to acknowledge their courage and would allow them to pass. If both Dian Wei and Guo Jia had their fates averted, Ziluan and Guo Jia instead prevent the fire attack by defeating Huang Gai though the bitter resistance from the allied forces forces Cao Cao's men to take a breather until their next campaign south.

If the Wanderer joins Sun Jian's forces, he will support Sun Jian's home voyage to Changsha before they are attacked by Kuai Liang on Yuan Shao's request to Liu Biao. Fortunately, the Wanderer receives reinforcements from Sun Ce and Sun Shangxiang as they defeat Huang Zu. With the new political scene centered between Yuan Shao and Yuan Shu, Sun Jian, having secretly acquired the Imperial Seal, opted to aid the latter, eking his way out until the seal could properly be returned to the Emperor. As Yuan Shu's ally, Sun Jian leads a campaign against Liu Biao, defeating Huang Zu at Fan Castle and cornering him at Xiangyang. Hoping to let his children take the lead, Sun Jian and Huang Gai take a support role for this final battle, but this backfires when Huang Zu's archers attack Sun Ce from the high ground. Sun Jian attempts to lead a counterattack but is trapped and wiped out by Lu Gong's archers instead.

Having lost his father, Sun Ce takes control of the clan and is forced to push them into Yuan Shu's service. As part of Yuan Shu's forces, the Wanderer and Sun Ce first move to defeat Liu Bei, a rival commander serving Tao Qian, an ally of Yuan Shao, as well as stopping Lu Kang's rebellion at Lujiang. When Yuan Shu reneges on his promise to make Sun Ce Lujiang's governor, Sun Ce chooses to follow his own vision rather than his father's. As a result, he yields the Imperial Seal to Yuan Shu in exchange for his father's men, and heads south to conquer their ancestral homeland of Wu.

Sun Ce's blazing campaigns win them many followers and territory as Sun Ce is recognized by Cao Cao during his call against Yuan Shu at Shouchun. Following Yuan Shu's defeat, Sun Ce attempts to launch an attack against Huang Zu at Xiakou but is forced to pull back by both rebellion at home, and the loss of Ling Cao's vanguard. The Wanderer joins Sun Quan in quelling the Shanyue, but this invokes the wrath of Bailuan, who serves as the Shanyue's guardian. Using a heavy miasma, Bailuan isolates Sun Ce after he is wounded by the late Xu Gong's men, and the warlord succumbs to his injuries while the Wanderer and Sun Quan are forced to retreat.

As Sun Quan was completely unproven, his initial rule focuses on him quelling rebellions at home, but Huang Gai begins showing signs of doubt of his young lord's rule. This comes to a head during the quelling of another Shanyue rebellion, where Huang Gai chooses to act independently from his lord, personally negotiating with a few leaders and convincing some to switch sides by himself. Sensing further conflict, Bailuan leads a final rebellion with the Shanyue against Sun Quan, and he is eventually defeated by Ziluan. To Bailuan's surprise, Sun Quan chooses to make peace with the Shanyue tribes, promising equal representation on matters of their land in exchange for their friendship. This results in Bailuan charging Ziluan to guarantee Sun Quan's promise before they part ways.

Having finally resolved matters in Wu, Ziluan helps Sun Quan launch a complete campaign against Huang Zu, surpassing his father and brother and defeating and slaying the commander. Just as things look up for Sun Quan, Cao Cao issues a warning against him as the warlord's expeditions now turn south against them. Choosing not to give up hope, Ziluan is sent to Liu Bei's forces to broker an alliance and plan against Cao Cao. Zhou Yu and Zhuge Liang later visit Ziluan to reveal that they had a secret plan to prepare a fire attack against Cao Cao, but needed time to prepare.

As a result, Ziluan joins the Wu vanguard at Sanjiangkou in skirmishing against Cao Cao's first wave. The Wu forces score a decisive victory against Cai Mao, but Huang Gai steps in and releases the general back to Cao Cao. This angers Zhou Yu who states that Cai Mao had been their agent, but Huang Gai's actions exposed him and resulted in him being executed instead. To punish Huang Gai, Zhou Yu has the veteran placed under house arrest, with only Ziluan arriving to speak with the commander over his decision. Huang Gai soon revealed his own jealousy over Sun Jian's fame and glory and felt that Sun Quan was completely unfit as leader. In the dead of night before the final battle at Chibi, Huang Gai, much to Ziluan's dismay, deserts the allied forces and joins Cao Cao.

During the final battle at Chibi, Ziluan aids the allied defense of the shoreline before both Huang Gai and Pang Tong suddenly switch sides and ensuring the complete success of the allied fire attack. Following the victory, Ziluan, Huang Gai, and others are personally thanked by Sun Quan, who is urged by the veterans to have more faith in himself. If both Sun Jian and Sun Ce's fates were averted, they will secretly travel to Xuchang with Bailuan's help and will seek an audience with Emperor Xian while Cao Cao's forces are preoccupied. This results in Emperor Xian abandoning Cao Cao as well and declaring the Sun family to be his true protectors.

In all kingdom endings, Ziluan's efforts earn him much fame as his lord considers assigning him as a general leading armies. Ziluan will have the option to accept this new post or to forego it for his freedom as an officer. Ziluan then meets up with Bailuan once more to reaffirm his convictions with the lord he selected and his commitment to bring peace through that lord.

In the true ending, Bailuan revisits the ruins of the Guardians of Peace and is joined by Bailuan. As they reminisce, they find that they still have the village's sacred jade pieces in hand, passed down by the village's chiefs and its strongest warrior. Ziluan then receives a mystery letter, sending him into a dream battle against the combined armies of Zhang Jiao, Yuan Shao, Yuan Shu, Lu Bu, and Dong Zhuo at Hulao Gate to test his might for future battles as the Guardian of Peace.

sections.heading: Voice Actors
sections.content:
- Jun Fukuyama (Japanese)

- John Patneaude (English)

- Zhang Boheng (Chinese)

sections.heading: Personality
sections.content:
Wanderer/Ziluan’s personality is quite reticent, a man of very few words (as said by Bailuan). He’s also an excellent listener whose calming eyes and demeanor seem to naturally invite others to be their true, vulnerable selves with him in a way they aren’t with anyone else.

Wanderer/Ziluan can be a little teasing with those whom he’s bonded with. But he’s caring and empathetic too. He has helped people in need even as an amnesiac. When Zhang Jiao passed away he allowed him to die and rest his body peacefully away from the eyes of brutal Han officials.

He can be confident, forthright, and is not intimidated by others but sees through to the heart of a matter. He’s able to bond even with intimidating, misunderstood figures like Yu Jin and Zhou Tai while earning the respect of beastly men like Dong Zhuo and Lu Bu.

sections.heading: Quotes
sections.content:
- "An opening!"

- "Now."

- "Take that!"

- "Time to act."

- "Must stay sharp."

- "What's next?"

- "Got this!"

- "Let's get serious."

- "Looking rough."

- "All my might!"

- "Let's end this."

- "A dire situation. Is there no way through...?"

- "The enemy officer has been defeated."

- "This territory is ours."
